method.hs_rss: ps -o rss= -p <pid> on macOS host (KB->MB) — virtual-memory RSS, excludes page cache
method.sb_rss: docker stats --no-stream sum over containers (cgroup memory.usage, includes page cache)
method.rss_asymmetry: cgroup memory.usage (SB) tends to run higher than ps RSS (HS) — favours HS. See FAIRNESS.md §F3.
method.sb_coldstart_note: Kong-restart only — other 9 containers remain warm. CONSERVATIVE LOWER BOUND. Full stack cold-start: 30-120s. See FAIRNESS.md §F2.
method.note: API-layer footprint excludes Postgres. Both HS and SB require a pg instance. Report shows API separately + Postgres separately.

api_layer.coldstart_note: Full API-layer cold-start: binary exec to first /ready HTTP 200
Kong-restart only (9 containers warm) — CONSERVATIVE LOWER BOUND; full stack cold-start 30-120s

ratios.honest_headline: minimal-prod ratio (skeptics who do not run Studio/analytics in prod are right to use this)
ratios.full_stack_for_completeness: full dev-stack ratio includes dev-only containers; reported for completeness not as headline

fairness_note: API-layer comparison: HyperStack binary vs Supabase API containers.
minimal-prod = Kong + GoTrue + PostgREST + Realtime + Storage (5 containers needed for production API traffic).
dev-extras = Studio + pg_meta + Vector + Analytics/Logflare + Inbucket (dev tooling, not required in production).
Postgres is NOT counted in either API-layer figure. Both need a pg instance.
HyperStack folds all API-layer functionality into a single binary (~3.9 MB).
RSS accounting: HS uses ps (macOS, excludes page cache); SB uses docker stats cgroup memory.usage (includes page cache). Different units — favours HS. See FAIRNESS.md §F3.
Cold-start: HS = single binary exec to first /ready 200 (FULL cold-start). SB = docker restart Kong only (CONSERVATIVE LOWER BOUND; full stack 30-120s). See FAIRNESS.md §F2.
